title: Prerelease Totem of Undying
description: Changes the Totem of Undying texture to the one seen at MINECON 2016 shortly before the item's reveal
body:
This resource pack changes the Totem of Undying texture to the one seen at MINECON 2016 shortly before the item's reveal.

I would have just...grabbed the image off the wiki and called it a day, but unfortunately the image there was enlarged to 128x128, and it wouldn't downscale properly on any image editing software I tried. So I did what any logical person would do and...recreated it by hand. Yep, that's right. Honestly, though, it was more fun that way, as I could have fun making something, as well as learn a little more about the Programmer Art style.

As with my previous resource pack, there are two versions. One is the normal version, and the other replaces the centre emerald with a ruby. This is for those who use [Ruby Emeralds](https://www.planetminecraft.com/texture-pack/ruby-emeralds-1-16/) and other similar mods.

As the texture itself was made when Programmer Art was still the primary art style for Minecraft, this is meant to be used with them. However, for those who use Jappa's textures but still wish to use the prerelease Totem design, I heavily recommend using [Unovian's reshade](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/prerelease-totem-remake). I'm not affiliated with them or anything; I just think it's really well done.

Enjoy!
